mapping.description:
Extracts the contents of Shell Bag structures stored in the `UsrClass` hive. These structures record directories and items that a user has browsed with Windows Explorer.
It reconstructs the hierarchical path of each bag entry, exposing the folder path, item type, name and associated registry metadata such as modification timestamps and security descriptors.
- Retrieves browsed folder paths from the registry. - Identifies the item type (root folder, file entry, network location, etc.).

field.description: displayed name of the shell item (folder, file, volume, etc.)
type identifier of the shell item (e.g., control_panel_category, network_location, file_entry)
localized (human‑readable) name of a file entry, if present in the extension block
NTFS file reference (MFT entry) of the file, formatted as 'MFT‑Sequence‑Index'
reconstructed hierarchical path derived from the shell bag entry
textual description associated with a network location item
user‑defined comments attached to a network location item
last modification timestamp of the underlying file
last access timestamp of the underlying file
creation timestamp of the underlying file
last modification timestamp of the registry key
SID of the user that owns the registry key
SID of the group that owns the registry key
security descriptor control flags for the key
SID of the account the ACE applies to
ACE flags that modify inheritance or behavior
permissions granted or denied by the ACE
